title.rendered: AMI Announces New Android Board Support Packages (BSP) for Intel® Customer Reference Boards

content.rendered:
NORCROSS, GEORGIA: – AMI, a global leader in BIOS and UEFI firmware, server and remote management tools, data storage products and unique solutions based on the Linux® and Android™ operating systems, is proud to announce that new Android Board Support Packages (BSP) for Intel® Customer Reference Boards are now available for download through the company’s website.

In addition to the previous Android BSP for Intel E3800 (“Bay Trail”) CRB, additional, new BSPs are now available for the Intel® Pentium®/Celeron® N3000 (“Braswell”/”Cherry Hill”) CRB and the Intel® Atom™ xx-Z8000 (“Cherry Trail”) CRB. Each of these Board Support Packages represents a complete, ready-to-use and recent version of the Android OS, bundled with a complete UEFI BIOS for testing, evaluation and software development purposes. More details on the specific versions of Android for each CRB, including release date and Android OS version number are available from the AMI website.

AMI President and CEO Subramonian Shankar noted that, “AMI is a leading provider of BIOS solutions and development support for Intel®-based hardware platforms. We also provide extensive support services for developers requiring assistance with Android development projects on Intel platforms, including, driver development, app development and OS customization services. Our expertise places AMI in a unique position to provide excellent support and assistance for customers’ Android development projects for Intel CRBs. We are standing by to support our OEM/ODM partners and other interested parties looking to get started with Android development for Intel hardware platforms.”

For more information on the background, solutions and resources that AMI provides for Android, please contact AMI at 1-800-828-9264.

This resource is provided in participation with the Intel® Internet of Things Systems Alliance. Intel®, Atom™, Pentium® and Celeron® are trademarks of Intel Corporation in the US and other countries. Android™ is a trademark of Google, Inc..
